summary: Package fetches payload from anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket
details: dist/index.js line 14 contains a hardcoded fetch() call to https://pub-0dc3e1677e894f07bbea11b17a29e032.r2.dev — an anonymous Cloudflare R2 bucket — combined with reads of process.platform and process.versions to select a platform-specific payload. Anonymous *.r2.dev buckets are throwaway, attacker-mutable hosting with no publisher identity; legitimate packages distribute native artifacts via versioned releases on the publisher's own GitHub or vendor CDN. The host shape matches known payload-distribution infrastructure used in recent npm dropper campaigns: a benign-looking shim that pulls a platform-targeted binary from an opaque bucket whose contents the maintainer can swap at any time. The fetched bytes are not pinned by hash, not hosted by the package's publisher, and not consistent with any documented runtime download pattern.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
